title: Arcytophyllum cephalanthum
taxonomy: Arcytophyllum cephalanthum is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The Rubiaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Arcytophyllum cephalanthum belonging to one of these groups. The exact classification of Arcytophyllum cephalanthum can be further specified into genus, subgenus, and species levels. It is classified within the subfamily Rubioideae, one of the largest subfamilies in the Rubiaceae family.

intro: Arcytophyllum cephalanthum, also known as Red Torchwood, is a plant species belonging to the family Rubiaceae, a family of flowering plants commonly known as the coffee or madder family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. Bioactive constituents of Arcytophyllum cephalanthum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are thought to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infused oil, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
